description: A pointer type that uniquely owns a heap allocation. Used for heap allocation, recursive types, and trait objects. No direct Python equivalent -- closest is a regular variable (Python always heap-allocates).

methods.name: new
methods.description: Allocates memory on the heap and places x into it.

methods.name: into_raw
methods.description: Consumes the Box and returns a raw pointer. The caller is responsible for freeing.

methods.name: from_raw
methods.description: Constructs a Box from a raw pointer. Unsafe because the pointer must be valid.

methods.name: leak
methods.description: Consumes the Box and leaks it, returning a mutable reference with static lifetime.
